title: Oncoclinicas Restructuring: IG4 Pursues Control Via Voting Shares
summary: IG4 Capital Group is seeking control of medical provider Oncoclinicas do Brasil Servicos Medicos SA by attempting to secure more than 50% of the voting rights, according to people familiar with the matter, a plan that would give the alternative asset manager a role in the rescue of another high-profile distressed company in Brazil.

title: Ford Commits $499 Million to Canada Engine Plant, Defends Trade Deal
summary: Ford Motor Co. and the union representing its Canadian employees ratified a contract that will mean wage increases and bonuses for thousands of workers, with the automaker committing to an investment in a southwestern Ontario engine plant.

title: Segro Rejects £13.5 Billion Cash and Share Prologis Offer
summary: Prologis Inc. has increased its outline bid to buy Segro Plc to £13.5 billion ($18.2 billion) and offered a series of sweeteners as it seeks to tempt shareholders after the UK landlord’s board rejected the latest terms.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 20
summary: Oil jumped after the US and Iran escalated hostilities, including the targeting of vessels attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz and an attack on an oil facility in Kuwait over the weekend.

title: Tungsten Miner’s Stock Jumps as Billionaire Forrest Buys Stake
summary: EQ Resources Ltd. shares surged after mining billionaire Andrew Forrest agreed to acquire a 16.8% stake in the Sydney-listed tungsten producer from Oaktree Capital Management LP.

title: Rising Demand for Hedges Hints at Volatility Ahead: Taking Stock
summary: The momentum trade carried along by the equity rally is faltering, while optimistic earnings expectations are under intense scrutiny, two of the signs pointing to a volatile summer.

title: Palm Climbs to Near One-Month High as Iran Conflict Boosts Crude
summary: Palm oil rose to the highest in nearly a month, tracking renewed strength in crude and other vegetable oil prices as the US and Iran exchanged escalating military strikes.

title: South Korea Details Plan to Make Won Freely Tradable Abroad
summary: South Korea laid out a detailed plan to make the won freely tradable among foreigners, its boldest step yet to liberalize the foreign-exchange market.

title: China Is Exporting 20% Fewer Magnets to US Despite Trade Truce
summary: China’s shipments of rare-earth magnets to the US remain well below pre-trade war levels despite last year’s truce, underscoring concerns in the Trump administration about Beijing’s adherence to that agreement.

title: Octopus-Backed MOPO Partners Nigeria in $75 Million Battery Plan
summary: MOPO, an Africa-focused battery rental company backed by Octopus Energy Group Ltd., said it’s signed an agreement with Nigeria to facilitate a $75 million expansion of its activities in Africa’s most populous nation.

title: Gold Steadies as Traders Assess Rates Path After US-Iran Clashes
summary: Gold steadied after the US and Iran ramped up attacks over the weekend, as traders assess the likelihood of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates to contain inflation.

title: Oil Rises as US-Iran Attacks Intensify, Bonds Fall: Markets Wrap
summary: Oil climbed to the highest level in more than a month and bonds fell as US and Iranian attacks escalated, renewing inflation concerns. Stocks steadied after a technology-led selloff rattled markets last week.

title: FTSE 100 Live: Bonds and Stocks Fall as Oil Prices Rise Back to $90
Safest Carry in Emerging Markets Is in Latin American Currencies
summary: Emerging-market currency volatility has dropped to the lowest since the start of year and that’s re-energizing carry trades, particularly those involving Latin America that are seen to offer the juiciest returns.

title: China Solar Stocks Rise After Beijing Issues Consumption Tax
summary: Shares of most Chinese solar and major battery makers rose on Monday after Beijing unveiled a consumption tax on solar cells and lithium batteries, raising hopes it will hasten industry consolidation, particularly in the oversupplied solar sector.

title: Emerging-Market Stocks Mixed as Investors Weigh Iran Tensions
summary: Emerging-market stocks and currencies were mixed as investors weighed rising tensions between the US and Iran, as well as a technology-led selloff that rattled global markets last week.

title: RBI Intervenes to Support Rupee as Currency Nears Record Low
summary: India’s central bank stepped into the foreign exchange markets to support the rupee after the currency weakened toward a record low as a renewed surge in crude prices weighed on sentiment.

title: GLP-1 Opportunity to Lift Domestic Business at Indian Pharma Firms
summary: A new and growing market for weight-loss drugs in India is expected to be a bright spot for pharmaceutical firms like Cipla Ltd. and Dr Reddy’s Laboratories Ltd., while they face headwinds in the US market.

title: Key China Copper Gauge Rallies to $100 After Tax Crackdown
summary: A closely-watched gauge of China’s copper market has risen to the highest in more than a year, as a tax shake-up in the metal’s biggest market spurs a shortage of scrap and boosts demand for imports.

title: NZD: Hedge Funds Amass Biggest New Zealand Dollar Net Short Since 2006
summary: Leveraged funds have taken their short positions on the New Zealand dollar to a record high, betting that a recent rebound in global oil prices may exacerbate domestic economic pressures.

title: US Strikes Iran in Escalating Campaign After Troops Killed
summary: The US and Iran engaged in a quickening series of tit-for-tat attacks, with the American military conducting a wave of strikes on the Islamic Republic following the deaths of service personnel.

title: Innolight Gauges Interest for $8 Billion Hong Kong Listing
summary: China’s Zhongji Innolight Co. is starting to gauge investor demand for its Hong Kong listing, according to terms of the deal seen by Bloomberg News, bringing it a step closer to what may be the city’s largest first-time share sale in seven years.
